question.text: Under the compulsory delisting notice requirements, the notice must be published in which combination of newspapers?

answer.text: One English national daily + one Hindi national newspaper (all India editions) + one regional language newspaper
Two English national dailies + one regional language newspaper
One English national daily + one regional language newspaper only
One English national daily + one Hindi national newspaper only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The delisting notice must be published in three newspapers: one English national daily, one Hindi national newspaper (both with all India editions), and one regional language newspaper. This ensures maximum reach across language groups. **The Trap:** Omitting the regional language newspaper is a common error. The regulation requires all three to ensure that even non-English, non-Hindi speaking shareholders in regional areas are informed. **Cross-Exam Context:** Newspaper publication requirements appear across multiple SEBI regulations (SAST, delisting, buyback) — the exact combination varies and is a frequent exam topic. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Stock exchanges must also display the notice on trading systems and their website — ensure all four channels (3 newspapers + exchange platform) are covered to avoid procedural challenges.
